subject: Cloud Computing
title: Serverless Limits and Tradeoffs
description: Learn Serverless Limits and Tradeoffs in one minute.

hook.text: Serverless sounds perfect until you hit a 15-minute timeout, a 250 MB package limit, or a cold start.

buildup.text: Every serverless platform imposes constraints: max memory, execution time, payload size, concurrency.

discovery.text: These limits exist because the provider must share resources fairly among millions of functions.

twist.text: Some workloads don't fit: long-running processes, WebSocket connections, or heavy stateful computation.

climax.text: Serverless is a tool, not a religion. Use it where it fits. Use servers where it doesn't.

punchline.text: Serverless isn't for everything. Knowing when to stop is the skill.

quiz.question: Why do serverless platforms have strict execution limits?
options: Resources must be shared fairly among millions of concurrent functions
Serverless hardware is physically weaker than regular servers
Cloud providers want to force customers to upgrade to IaaS
